sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Level 1 completed! Now on Level 2. Let me inspect the new board to understand the layout.
From the images, I can see:
- A light gray vertical divider in the middle
- Left side: a charcoal L-shape and a yellow L-shape
- Right side: a black L-shape (with white dots inside - this is likely the controllable piece)
The pattern from Level 1 was: move the black shape to overlap the charcoal shape (same shape, different color). Let me verify this by inspecting the current frame's segmentation.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print("Level:", current_frame.level)
print("Nodes:")
for n in nodes:
    print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} hash={n['hash']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
print("\nAdjacency:")
for a in seg['adjacency_list']:
    print(f"  {a}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Level 2 analysis:
- Background: blue (id=0, left), blue (id=2, right)
- Divider: light gray vertical strip (id=1, col 36-38) with white dots
- Charcoal L-shape: id=10, 72 pixels, boundary around rows 18-29, cols 15-29 (left side)
- Black L-shape: id=11, 64 pixels, boundary around rows 18-29, cols 45-59 (right side) - this is the controllable piece
- Yellow L-shape: id=28, 72 pixels, boundary around rows 42-53, cols 3-17 (left side)
- The black L-shape has blue dots inside (ids 13-23)
The goal from Level 1 was to move the black shape to overlap the charcoal shape. The charcoal shape (id=10) is on the left side, and the black shape (id=11) is on the right side. I need to move the black shape LEFT to overlap the charcoal shape.
The charcoal L-shape boundary: [[18, 21], [18, 29], [20, 29], [20, 24], [21, 23], [29, 23], [29, 15], [27, 15], [27, 20], [26, 21]]
The black L-shape boundary: [[18, 45], [18, 53], [26, 53], [27, 54], [27, 59], [29, 59], [29, 51], [21, 51], [20, 50], [20, 45]]
Looking at the boundaries, the charcoal L is at rows 18-29, cols 15-29. The black L is at rows 18-29, cols 45-59. They're at the same row range but different columns. The black shape needs to move left by about 45-15=30 columns to overlap.
But wait - there's a divider at col 36-38. The black shape is on the right (col 45+) and the charcoal shape is on the left (col 15-29). The divider might block movement. Let me try moving left and see what happens.
Actually, looking more carefully at the shapes:
- Charcoal L (id=10): 72 pixels - this seems like a larger L shape
- Yellow L (id=28): 72 pixels - same size
- Black L (id=11): 64 pixels - slightly smaller
The charcoal and yellow shapes have the same pixel count (72) but different hashes, so they might be different orientations of the same shape. The black shape has 64 pixels.
Let me just try moving the black shape left to overlap the charcoal shape, following the Level 1 pattern.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move black L-shape left to overlap the charcoal L-shape
# Black L is at cols 45-59, charcoal L is at cols 15-29
# Need to move left by about 30 columns to align
# But there's a divider at col 36-38 that might block
# Let's try moving left and see what happens
action(['LEFT'] * 30)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
